focus: [D2-6/D2] command-as-mode vs command-as-task framing fork - should a /design:* command address an internal mode or a user intent?

graphEvents.label: Pinned design command wrappers expose internal mode language as the public command frame
mode-registry is already the internal workflowMode/backendKind binding map
impeccable command docs lead with user jobs and categories before internal phases
commandSurface should separate userIntent from modeBinding
A static checker can prevent bridge-first mode copy in pinned command wrappers

label: Pinned design command wrappers expose internal mode language as the public command frame
mode-registry is already the internal workflowMode/backendKind binding map
impeccable command docs lead with user jobs and categories before internal phases
commandSurface should separate userIntent from modeBinding
A static checker can prevent bridge-first mode copy in pinned command wrappers
Pinned design commands expose internal mode language before user jobs
enforceability: ENFORCEABLE for static wrapper copy and replay fixtures; ADVISORY for ambiguous live prompts

label: Registry should remain the internal binding while command metadata owns user-intent projection
enforceability: ENFORCEABLE for schema separation, wrapper drift, and router stability

label: impeccable-main supports task/job command framing over public mode-key framing
enforceability: PARTLY ENFORCEABLE for docs shape and aliases; ADVISORY for final naming taste

label: Command descriptions must lead with user intent; internal mode binding belongs in generated metadata or an explicit internal-binding section
All five checked /design:* wrappers use thin-bridge, pin-mode, load-mode, or apply-mode phrasing

direction: Rename existing /design:* commands before adding metadata
reason: Compatibility risk is unnecessary; existing mode-named wrappers can be preserved while generated copy and optional aliases move the public frame to user intent.

direction: Put user-facing command framing only in child mode SKILL.md files
reason: Pinned slash-command wrappers are the execution surface, so hidden child-skill wording is too late to prevent command-as-mode routing mistakes.
